Ad Text:
Make a meaningful difference with us:

We want people who are great as individuals, and even better in a team.

We’re on the lookout for a strategic and hands-on Solution Analysis & Design Manager, who’s ready to roll up their sleeves and lead the delivery of high-quality solution analysis and project-level solution architecture across enterprise-wide digital and technology initiatives. Operating within our Digital & Technology (D&T) Transformation function, this role is responsible for ensuring solution designs are aligned with Enterprise Architecture principles, governance standards and UnitingCare’s 2030 Strategy.

This role will lead and guide project and program analysis and design resources (including Solution Architects, Business Analysts, technical leads and vendors), ensuring design standards, guardrails and assurance processes are consistently applied to deliver sustainable, customer-centric technology outcomes.

Key Responsibilities

Own the delivery of project-level solution architecture and detailed design execution for approved initiatives, ensuring solution quality, technical coherence and alignment with delivery plans.

Ensure all solution designs are reviewed through established architecture governance and design assurance forums, addressing feedback and demonstrating compliance with enterprise architecture principles and standards.

Lead and guide D&T project and program analysis and design resources with respect to scope, deliverables, design artefacts, review processes and expected outcomes.

Partner with the Enterprise Architecture Manager to support demand intake, triage and early assessment activities, contributing feasibility input and delivery insights to inform investment decisions.

Develop and implement analysis and design standards, templates and guidance to ensure fit-for-purpose, customer-centric solution outcomes.

Drive continuous improvement of technical delivery methods, reference architectures, cost estimation models, policies and standards.

Maintain oversight of solution architecture capacity, demand and performance reporting.

Prepare senior-level presentations and reports to align stakeholders and drive informed decision-making.

Analyse industry trends, product lifecycles and technology roadmaps to support sustainable digital blueprint delivery.

Drive successful implementation of D&T initiatives by translating between technology and business stakeholders.

 

What you will bring:

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Management or related discipline (or equivalent industry experience).

7+ years’ experience leading a solution analysis and design capability within complex, multi-vendor environments.

Extensive experience in business requirements gathering, high-level and detailed systems design, and implementable architecture patterns.

Demonstrated experience establishing and maintaining solution governance and design assurance processes.

Strong background across solution architecture, business analysis and/or software development.

Exceptional analytical, conceptual and problem-solving skills.

Proven ability to engage executive and technical stakeholders with high-quality presentations, reports and documentation.

Experience working across SaaS platforms, cloud (Azure), integration environments and multi-vendor ecosystems highly regarded.

Relevant certifications (TOGAF, ITIL, Agile or vendor certifications) desirable.

A collaborative leadership style, strong mentoring capability and commitment to developing technical talent.

Commitment to the mission and values of the not-for-profit sector.

 

Why you will love it here:

Working with us means stepping into a role that’s as broad as it is meaningful. You will gain exposure to:

Multiple industries under one roof - health, aged care, disability, community services, and more.

A wide range of workforce models, leadership styles, and organisational challenges.

Opportunities to stretch, grow, and shape your career in ways you won’t find elsewhere.

 

And the perks? We’ve got plenty:

Flexible work arrangements to support work-life balance.

Access to salary packaging, meal and entertainment benefits - up to $15,900 per year tax free as well as up to $2,650 of your pre-tax income to pay for meal and entertainment benefits. To learn more about this benefit watch the video via the following link: Salary Packaging Explained.     

A recognition and rewards platform, providing employee discounts at hundreds of retailers, including everyday expenses, holidays, health insurance, a wellbeing program and employee assistance program.

Career development opportunities to challenge yourself, grow and make a meaningful difference.

12 weeks paid parental leave and 2 weeks paid partner leave, available after 6 months service, in addition to the government scheme.

Diversity and inclusive leave, offering paid gender affirmation leave and cultural leave options (paid and unpaid) for those who may celebrate Lunar New Year, Diwali, Ramadan, NAIDOC weeks and other significant cultural events.

We’re committed to creating safe, inclusive, and welcoming environments for all—especially children, young people, and the elderly. Our services are built on trust, care, and a deep sense of responsibility.

Ready to bring your whole self to work and make a real difference? Apply now and join a team where your work truly matters.

Manager, Customer Insights

Clerk Grade: 11/12

Annual Salary Range: $149,739 - $173,174 plus superannuation

Employment Type: Ongoing, Full-time

Location: Sydney, McKell Building

About the team

In Customer Insights & Experience (CIE), our focus is ensuring the voices of people, communities, and businesses sit at the centre of decision-making and service delivery across NSW Government.

We help government understand what communities need, think, and experience - from detailed service interactions to broader community sentiment - and turn those insights into better decisions and better services.

You'll join a passionate team of specialists, with expertise in research, insights, customer experience, and service design. Together, we deliver actionable insights and practical improvements that shape policy, investment, communications, and service delivery across the whole of NSW Government - including departments, agencies, Ministers and Cabinet.

We generate actionable insights. Our insights specialists provide a deep understanding of the people, communities, and businesses to inform government decisions and identify opportunities to improve outcomes.
We turn insights into action. Our customer experience and service design specialists apply human-centered design, co-design, usability testing, and service transformation methods to create, refine, and uplift service experiences, ensuring tangible improvements for the people of NSW.

Your day-to-day

As a Manager, Customer Insights, you will play a lead role in the design, execution, and delivery of key research and insights projects - managing a team of research and insights specialists and working with key stakeholders to deliver actionable insights that inform government decision-making and service delivery.

This role requires a leader who can combine technical research expertise with strategic thinking, stakeholder engagement and team development. You will guide your team through rigorous research design, fieldwork, data analysis, insights development and presentations, ensuring insights are simple, compelling, and actionable.

In this role, you will:

Lead and manage a team of insights specialists, providing coaching and professional development to uplift capability and ensure consistent delivery of high-quality insights.
Oversee a portfolio of concurrent research and insights projects - including BAU reporting, whole-of-government insights programs, and bespoke research addressing agency-specific needs.
Proactively identify, leverage and integrate numerous datasets to develop insights reports with simple, clear, and actionable insights to stakeholders across all levels of government.
Engage with stakeholders across NSW Government to understand challenges, clarify objectives, scope work and translate needs into well-designed, costed insights proposals.
Lead the design, delivery, and execution of ad-hoc quantitative and qualitative insights projects to address key objectives and inform government decision-making, including research design, questionnaire design, discussion guide development, fieldwork or supplier management, data analysis, insights reporting, and presentations.
Deliver clear, actionable insights through reports, dashboards, presentations and workshops that support evidence-based decisions across policy, investment, communications, and service design and delivery.

To be successful in this role you will demonstrate:

Extensive experience leading complex research and insights projects end-to-end, across qualitative and quantitative methods, sampling design, fieldwork, data analysis and insights communication.
Strong consulting capabilities - able to engage with senior stakeholders, translate ambiguous or high-level questions into structured research briefs, and design methodological approaches that deliver clear and actionable insights.
A proven ability to synthesise complex data into meaningful, actionable insights and communicate them effectively through written reports, visualisations, presentations and facilitated workshops.
Strong people leadership experience, including coaching, performance management, capability uplift and fostering a culture of excellence in insights and storytelling.
Strong project and portfolio management skills, with the ability to balance multiple priorities, maintain high quality standards and meet deadlines in a fast‑paced environment.
Deep understanding of customer insights, community sentiment and customer experience measurement programs - ideally within large, complex or public sector contexts.
Highly developed interpersonal and influencing skills, with the ability to build trust, manage expectations and partner effectively across diverse teams and executive levels.
Experience with Q Research Software.
Strong proficiency in the Microsoft suite (PowerPoint, Excel, Word).

What we need from you:

Please click on the link and attach your resume (max 5 pages) and cover letter (max 2 pages). In your cover letter please share your motivation for applying for this position and your relevant skills.

Please note candidates invited to interview will be required to complete additional assessments.

A talent pool may be created from this recruitment process to fill future ongoing and temporary opportunities.

Salary Grade 11/12, with the base salary for this role starting at $149,739 base plus superannuation

Join an organisation with an unbreakable belief that quality education, training and research should be available to everyone – regardless of background, location or life circumstances.

Working at CQUniversity

The Students Directorate is responsible for enhancing the student experience and success across the University, including the delivery of advising services, student support initiatives, and strategic programs that improve retention, engagement, and outcomes.

As a key leader within Student Advising, you will play a pivotal role in shaping a consistent, high-quality advising model across CQUniversity. You will analyse advising data and student insights to drive continuous improvement, collaborate with stakeholders to embed best practice, and support change initiatives that strengthen the student success ecosystem.

Fantastic professional and career development opportunities

Access to an Employee Assistance Program and Fitness Passport

Proven ability to analyse data and insights to inform decision-making, continuous improvement and strategic outcomes.

Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills, with the ability to influence practice across diverse teams.

Experience developing capability uplift initiatives, including training, resources or professional learning programs.

High-level organisational and problem-solving skills, with the ability to manage competing priorities and deliver quality outcomes.

Ad Text:
ASIS relies on innovative and secure ICT solutions to achieve its national security mission. ICT Service Delivery Management is central to this effort, supporting the strategy, planning, and delivery of ICT products from initial concept to transition into service.

The role defines, delivers, and manages projects that meet ASIS’s operational requirements, security standards, and contribute directly to enhanced intelligence capabilities.

Role Responsibilities:

The IT Delivery role will generally be required to undertake work that is very complex or sensitive and operate under broad direction.
They will exercise a considerable degree of independence and perform a leadership role.
They will be required to exercise sound decision making and judgement to produce high level policy advice.
The IT Delivery role will be required to engage in complex problem solving and issues management and may coordinate and undertake detailed or sensitive projects that impact on strategic, political or operational outcomes for the agency.
They will be required to actively manage key stakeholder relationships within and outside the agency and may manage one or more work teams.

We encourage applicants with one or more of the following skills, attributes or experience to apply:

Global Rollout Support Planning
Project, Asset and Logistics Management
Collaboration
Outcomes Driven

The following education, qualifications and/or experience will be highly regarded:

Experience in project management – certifications or demonstrated history are desirable.
Certifications such as ITIL are desirable.
Familiarity with ICT transformation initiatives.
Experience managing or supporting large projects.
Knowledge of ICT hardware is desirable.
Awareness of how ICT systems fit together is desirable.
Eligibility

To be eligible for a role you must:

Be an Australian citizen
Be assessed as suitable to hold and maintain a TOP SECRET-Privileged Access security clearance

For more information on eligibility please see the Protective Security Policy Framework which is publicly accessible at https://protectivesecurity.gov.au, section 12 provides information on Eligibility and suitability.

The position is Canberra based, with conditions similar to those in the Australian Public service including superannuation.

Company description:



About Newmont

Newmont is the world's leading gold company, producing gold as well as copper, silver, zinc, lead and molybdenum across operations in Australia, Papua New Guinea, Africa, Latin America and North America. In Australia, Newmont operates across Western Australia, the Northern Territory and New South Wales, supported by teams in Perth, Brisbane and Melbourne.

At Newmont, people create the value in the metals we mine and are ambassadors in the communities where they live and work. As a team, we foster an environment in support of our people vision, where everyone belongs, thrives and is valued. We have built a diverse and inclusive workplace where our people can bring their differences to support delivering results together.

Join us and unearth your potential in a global company that is at the forefront of shaping the future of the mining industry.



Job description:



Your impact
Reporting to the Data Architecture Lead, you will support the development and evolution of Newmont's enterprise data platform by designing and delivering scalable data and AI solutions.

You will work with large, complex datasets to build and deploy modern data pipelines, machine learning and AI-enabled solutions that solve real business problems. This role sits at the intersection of data engineering, data science and platform delivery, partnering closely with architecture, engineering and business teams to deliver secure, scalable and repeatable solutions.


As part of our team you will

Design, build and deploy data pipelines and AI/ML-enabled solutions using cloud-based platforms
Work with large structured and unstructured datasets to support enterprise data use cases
Translate solution design into executable technical delivery in an agile environment
Contribute to the standardisation of architecture and deployment patterns
Support the development of scalable, secure and repeatable data solutions
Apply and embed data governance, data quality and ethical AI practices
Collaborate with AI/ML, Digital Enablement and operational teams to solve business problems
Support end-to-end data workflows including ingestion, transformation and consumption
Optimise data processes and models for performance, scalability and reliability
Contribute to continuous improvement of the enterprise data platform and data capabilities


Profile description:



Prerequisites for your success in the role

Degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics or related field
Strong SQL capability (essential), including experience working with large datasets
Experience with Python for data engineering or machine learning use cases
Experience working with cloud-based data platforms (e.g. Databricks, Azure, AWS or similar)
Understanding of data governance, data quality and data management practices
Ability to translate business requirements into clear technical solutions
Experience working with enterprise-scale data or complex data environments

Well regarded:

Experience with Databricks or similar lakehouse platforms
Experience with large-scale data processing (e.g. Spark)
Exposure to machine learning frameworks (e.g. TensorFlow, PyTorch)
Experience with APIs and system integration in enterprise environments
Experience with GenAI, LLMs, or modern AI solution design (e.g. RAG, vector databases)
Experience in mining, resources, or heavy industry environments
Understanding of operational data and value chains

Your work environment
This role is ideally based in Perth, however flexible work arrangements, including remote options, will be considered in line with business and operational requirements.

You will work closely with a highly capable technical team across architecture, engineering and data functions, supporting stakeholders across operations and digital teams.

Occasional travel may be required to engage with stakeholders or support specific initiatives.

Why Join Us:

Work on a high-impact transformation program
Collaborate with experienced project and technology teams
Opportunity to shape future-state systems and business processes

This role is 5 days in office.

Join one of Australia's Big 4 Banks as an AI data Scientist in a new enterprise AI function of the bank. This is a rare opportunity to work at the forefront of applied AI while helping shape the next generation of banking technology.

These are some of the most sophisticated AI systems being built in financial services today. They demand cutting-edge thinking in agent architecture, evaluation methodology, and safety engineering, where reliability is critical and mistakes have real-world financial impact.

You'll work closely with the Head of Data Science and collaborate within small, highly technical squads where experimentation, rapid decision-making, and intellectual honesty are valued more than hierarchy or bureaucracy.

Key Responsibilities

Design and build agentic AI systems, including reasoning loops, orchestration logic, tool integrations, memory frameworks, and context management.
Translate AI architecture into production-ready solutions, bridging the gap between data science experimentation and scalable engineering implementation.
Develop prompting strategies and retrieval pipelines, while running rapid experimentation to improve agent performance and reliability.
Implement evaluation, monitoring, and safety frameworks to detect issues such as hallucinations, drift, or unexpected agent behaviour and ensure systems operate within defined guardrails.
Strengthen the broader AI capability by building reusable tools, sharing knowledge across teams, and mentoring junior data scientists.

Skills & Experience

Degree in a quantitative discipline such as computer science, machine learning, statistics, mathematics, physics, or engineering.
Proven experience delivering AI or machine learning systems into production environments.
Hands-on experience across machine learning and applied AI, with strong depth in areas such as NLP, recommender systems, ML modelling, or LLM-based applications.
Strong Python development skills and experience working with modern ML and AI ecosystems.
Practical experience across the ML lifecycle: data exploration, feature engineering, model development, evaluation, experimentation, and deployment.
Exposure to foundation model APIs, prompt engineering, embeddings, retrieval techniques, or contextual data pipelines.
Strong grounding in statistics, probability, and experimental design to rigorously evaluate AI models and systems.

To be eligible for this position, candidates must have full working rights in Australia.

Job Title: Director - Payroll Remediation and Data Analytics
Location: Sydney
Work Type: Full-time
Working Arrangement: Hybrid, with an expectation of approximately three days per week in the office and/or on client sites
Salary: $220,000-$250,000 inclusive of super, plus bonus incentives

The Opportunity We are seeking a Director-level professional to join a high-performing advisory team focused on payroll remediation, wage compliance, and data-led compliance engagements.

This role is suited to a senior leader who combines deep payroll remediation and wage compliance expertise with strong data analytics capability. You will lead complex engagements for large employers with sophisticated payroll environments, providing strategic advice, overseeing technical workstreams, and driving commercially sound outcomes for clients.

There is an established pipeline of work in place, so this role offers immediate delivery impact while also requiring broader Director-level capability across client leadership, team development, commercial acumen, and business growth.

Key Responsibilities
Lead complex payroll remediation, wage compliance, and data-led compliance engagements from discovery through to remediation and implementation support
Act as a senior advisor to clients on payroll compliance, remediation strategy, risk, governance, and delivery approach
Oversee the design and application of data models used to identify underpayments, assess historical liabilities, and support remediation outcomes
Translate awards, enterprise agreements, overtime, penalties, superannuation, and related obligations into practical and defensible remediation approaches
Review and sign off on technical outputs and client deliverables, ensuring quality and commercial practicality
Lead multiple concurrent engagements, balancing client outcomes, delivery quality, utilisation, and margin
Manage senior client stakeholders and help shape the direction of engagements
Lead, mentor, and develop multi-level teams across Senior Managers, Managers, Senior Consultants, and Consultants
Support engagement scoping, pricing, resource planning, and broader commercial decision-making
Contribute to growth through relationship development, cross-functional collaboration, and identification of new opportunities
Candidate Profile To be successful in this role, you will bring:

Significant experience in payroll remediation, wage compliance, or related workforce compliance programs
Strong advanced analytics capability across payroll or workforce datasets
Ability to translate legislative, industrial, or payroll complexity into clear calculation logic and practical client outcomes
Proven experience leading sensitive, high-impact engagements in complex environments
Strong commercial judgement and confidence operating with senior stakeholders
Demonstrated team leadership capability, including mentoring and managing multi-level delivery teams
Ability to operate at Director level across both delivery and commercial leadership
Key Requirements
Deep payroll remediation / wage compliance experience
Advanced analytics capability, ideally including SQL, Python, Power BI, or similar tools
Experience working with large payroll, workforce, or HR datasets
Strong understanding of awards, enterprise agreements, overtime, penalties, super, and related compliance considerations
Experience in advisory, consulting, or a similarly commercial environment
Strong client-facing capability and executive-level communication skills
Experience contributing to business development, account growth, or practice development
What Success Looks Like Success in this role will be reflected through:

High-quality delivery of complex payroll remediation and compliance engagements
Trusted senior stakeholder relationships and strong client outcomes
Effective leadership of multi-level teams across multiple workstreams
Strong commercial discipline across delivery, resourcing, and margin
Contribution to growth through relationship development and new opportunity identification
Summary This is a rare opportunity for a senior payroll remediation and analytics professional to step into a visible Director-level role, leading complex client work while helping shape capability, commercial outcomes, and future growth.

About us

Founded in 2020, the team at The Lookout Way set out to transform the delivery of in-home care by blending cutting-edge technology with deep industry insight. Born from firsthand experience with the challenges facing Aged Care providers, we built the only digital care management platform created by care, for care.

Our flagship platform, Lookout, is an all-in-one SaaS solution that empowers providers and their clients with the tools and visibility needed to deliver high-quality, profitable care. With a strong focus on intuitive design, it connects staff, care workers, and clients through personalised interfaces tailored to each user’s role.

About the Role

As our Project Delivery Lead, you won’t just be "managing tasks"—you will be the strategic architect behind the end-to-end implementation of the Lookout Care Suite for our largest, most complex customers. You will bridge the gap between technical system architecture and executive-level business goals, ensuring that our partners in the Aged Care and NDIS sectors aren’t just installing software, but undergoing a successful digital transformation.

This is a high-visibility role requiring a rare blend of technical depth and "C-suite ready" communication. Your mission is to lead cross-functional teams to deliver best-in-class customer experiences on time and within budget.

The core responsibilities of the role include:

Define scope, goals, and deliverables for large-scale implementations, managing detailed project plans and budgets.
Establish deep-rooted relationships with executive leadership and boards, providing high-level governance reporting and proactive risk mitigation.
Become an expert in the Lookout product ecosystem. You’ll translate complex system architecture (APIs, BI tools, and integrations) into sequenced, actionable deliverables.
Facilitate high-impact workshops and discovery sessions to identify opportunities for operational uplift and value activation.

Do you have what it takes?

You are a big picture thinker who never loses sight of the details. You understand that successful system onboarding is 20% technology and 80% people and process, and you thrive in environments where change needs to be practical, scalable, and well‑managed across the organisation.  

5+ years’ experience delivering software implementations within mid‑sized organisations (e.g. SaaS vendors, agencies) leading projects that balance enterprise‑grade rigor with real‑world operational constraints. 
Strong understanding of system design and the SDLC, with hands‑on experience integrating HRIS, Finance, and Payroll systems in organisations where systems, teams, and budgets must work closely together. 
Experience in Aged Care and/or NDIS environments is highly regarded, particularly within providers where regulatory compliance, rostering, clinical delivery, and financial processes intersect daily.  
Highly developed emotional intelligence, with the ability to translate technical concepts into clear, practical language for non‑technical stakeholders across diverse teams.  
Proven experience managing and delivering projects within defined timeframes, working with cross functional teams where adaptability and collaboration are critical to success. 
Ability to obtain and maintain clear, relevant criminal history checks.  
Unlimited working rights in Australia. 

What's in it for you?

The way we work and the perks we offer are designed to help you live a fulfilling life. The work we do is meaningful, and the problems we solve are interesting.

A competitive salary package that will recognise your skills and experience.
You’ll work remotely from anywhere in Australia as long as you have a stable internet connection and a comfortable setup. We do have offices throughout the country for face to face contact.
Annual $500 health and well-being allowance to spend on anything contributing to your health and well-being away from work.
On top of the standard 4 weeks of annual leave, after your second year, you will accumulate an additional day of annual leave each year you are with us. You will also have access to our Pandemic Leave policy and have the chance to purchase additional annual leave.
Access to our employee assistance programme, where you can receive free confidential and professional counselling.
Parental leave benefits –All caregivers who are eligible for the government’s paid parental leave scheme will receive top-up wage payments. Additionally, primary caregivers will receive top-up superannuation payments for their first year of unpaid parental leave.
Extend the Five Good Friends care to a loved one at a special rate. The nominated family member will receive Care Managed services at Self-Managed prices (values vary depending on circumstances).
Working for a company that has an employee engagement score that is 17% higher than the industry average, and 7% higher than the top 25% industry average (we are actually a great place to work).
And… after 5 years of continuous service, you will receive a trip to Okinawa, Japan - the community that inspired the creation of Five Good Friends. Costs of your return economy airfare, 5 nights in hotel accommodation and airport transfers will be paid.

Find out more about The Lookout Way here:

https://www.thelookoutway.com

How to Apply

If this role sounds like you, please click on the apply button and follow the prompts. We will get in touch if you are shortlisted for this opportunity. Please include your CV and a cover letter in your application, telling us a bit about yourself and your suitability for this role within our organisation.

We respectfully request no communication from external agencies or recruiters.

Based at our Geelong, Waterfront Campus or Melbourne, Burwood Campus + hybrid work arrangements
Full-time (36.75 hours per week) and fixed term up to 5 years
Competitive Remuneration + 17% Superannuation 

Why work with us?

At Deakin you will have access to benefits such as a variety of leave options including generous parental leave and the ability to purchase additional leave; flexible working arrangements to help manage your work-life balance; ongoing learning and development opportunities to grow your career; an inclusive and supportive culture and environment to work in, both online and on campus.

This influential role drives strategic academic priorities aligned with Deakin’s Education and Employability Plan 2025–2030. We're seeking a forward-thinking expert with deep knowledge of the higher education landscape and a passion for innovation and impact.

As the Senior Strategic Adviser, you will:

 PD - Senior Strategic Adviser.pdf

Are You Ready?

Deakin is a Victorian university with a global impact. We are an agile, dynamic, and innovative university committed to making a positive impact through our excellence in education, research and innovation and the contributions we make to the wider community.

We understand that our reputation has been built on the dedication and expertise of our staff and we offer a dynamic and diverse working environment with opportunities to grow and develop careers. We value diversity and aim to build an inclusive environment that champions, embraces and respects differences. We support and encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, and people of all abilities, cultures, sexual orientation, and genders.

Pre-employment safety and suitability checks: In accordance with the National Higher Education Code to Prevent and Respond to Gender-based Violence, appointment to this role is subject to successful completion of relevant pre-employment checks, including Working With Children Checks and candidate gender-based violence declarations.

Ad Text:
MTC is a global leader in corrections, immigration detention, education, and workforce development, with a proud mission to transform lives and strengthen communities. Our people are at the heart of everything we do - and we’re proud of the impact we make. 

About The Role 

Lead intelligence operations at Melbourne Immigration Detention Centre (MIDC), located at Broadmeadows. Reporting to the Centre Manager, you’ll be the central point for intelligence gathering, analysis, and reporting. Your work will help prevent incidents, manage risks, and support operational decisions in a complex, high-turnover environment.

You’ll collaborate with operational teams, the National Intelligence team, and external agencies to drive intelligence-led decision-making and deliver safer, more informed outcomes across multiple detention sites.

This role offers variety, complexity and purpose. It is ideal for someone who thrives in dynamic environments and adapts quickly.

You’ll lead a small team, with one direct report (Intelligence Analyst), and provide pastoral care to the Risk Analyst team.

Key Responsibilities: 

Lead intelligence operations across MIDC.

Collect, analyse, and disseminate intelligence related to detainee behaviour, contraband, threats, and external risks.

Produce timely, actionable intelligence reports to inform operational decisions.

Collaborate with internal teams and external stakeholders (ABF, VicPol, AFP).

Monitor behavioural trends and contribute to proactive risk mitigation.

Support staff training in intelligence awareness and secure information handling.

Contribute to incident response planning and intelligence policy development

To be successful: 

Cert IV in Security Operations or Technical Security, or equivalent (mandatory)

Significant experience in intelligence, investigations, or security analysis, ideally within a detention, correction, law enforcement, or defence setting. 

Strong understanding of intelligence principles, risk identification, and threat analysis. 

Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to influence and collaborate across all levels.  

Flexibility to respond to critical incidents outside standard work hours

Ability to obtain and maintain security clearance 

Must be double vaccinated against COVID-19 

Ready to Join Us? 

For more information about the role please email melissa.sloane@mtctrains.com.au

Manager, Customer Insights (Have Your Say)

Clerk Grade: 11/12

Annual Salary Range: $149,739 - $173,174 plus superannuation

Employment Type: Ongoing, Full-time

Location: Sydney, McKell Building

About the team

In Customer Insights & Experience (CIE), our focus is ensuring the voices of people, communities, and businesses sit at the centre of decision-making and service delivery across NSW Government.

We help government understand what communities need, think, and experience - from detailed service interactions to broader community sentiment - and turn those insights into better decisions and better services.

You'll join a passionate team of specialists, with expertise in research, insights, customer experience, and service design. Together, we deliver actionable insights and practical improvements that shape policy, investment, communications, and service delivery across the whole of NSW Government - including departments, agencies, Ministers and Cabinet.

We generate actionable insights. Our insights specialists provide a deep understanding of the people, communities, and businesses to inform government decisions and identify opportunities to improve outcomes.
We turn insights into action. Our customer experience and service design specialists apply human-centered design, co-design, usability testing, and service transformation methods to create, refine, and uplift service experiences, ensuring tangible improvements for the people of NSW.

About Have Your Say

Have Your Say is NSW Government's central hub for public consultation, where communities can share their views on government projects, policies, services and reforms. It provides a single, statewide listing of all public consultations currently open for comment, ensuring transparency and enabling people to easily find and participate in engagement opportunities.

Your day-to-day

As the Manager, Customer Insights (Have Your Say), you will lead a team responsible for designing, executing and reporting on major community consultation research projects. You will ensure that insights from the public - including diverse voices across regional, urban, vulnerable and culturally diverse communities - directly inform NSW Government decision-making.

You will drive the end-to-end delivery of consultation research, working closely with key stakeholders and specialised teams to ensure research is robust, inclusive, accessible and aligned with NSW Government standards. This includes strong expertise in community consultation methodologies, questionnaire design, fieldwork management, data analysis, insights reporting and stakeholder engagement.

You will act as a strategic advisor across NSW Government, helping agencies scope consultation needs, design methodological approaches and deliver insights that shape policy, legislation, reform programs and service improvements.

In this role, you will:

Lead, coach and develop a team of insights specialists delivering high-quality community consultation research across the Have Your Say program.
Design, manage and deliver major consultation studies, including methodology development, survey and questionnaire design, sampling, recruitment, fieldwork, analysis, and insights reporting.
Collaborate with key internal teams and external stakeholders to ensure consultations are delivered to a high-standard, address key objectives, whilst meeting accessibility, inclusivity and governance standards.
Produce compelling, actionable insights through reports, dashboards, executive presentations and interactive workshops that influence decision-making.
Guide agencies on best‑practice consultation processes, including how to publish, promote and manage consultations on the Have Your Say platform.
Ensure privacy, data governance and ethical considerations are embedded in all consultation activities in line with NSW Government requirements.

To be successful in this role you will demonstrate:

Extensive experience leading end-to-end research and community consultations, including questionnaire design, qualitative and quantitative methods, sampling, recruitment and fieldwork management.
Strong data analysis and storytelling capability - able to translate complex consultation data into meaningful and actionable insights for senior decision-makers.
Demonstrated experience managing multiple research projects concurrently, with high attention to quality, accuracy and timeliness.
Strong stakeholder engagement and consulting skills, including working with senior executives, policy leaders and communications teams to scope, design and deliver consultation programs.
Strong people leadership experience, including coaching, performance management, capability uplift and fostering a culture of excellence in insights and storytelling.
Strong understanding of NSW Government consultation requirements, and adherence to accessibility and plain‑English standards.
A commitment to community-centred decision-making and ensuring people across NSW have meaningful opportunities to participate in shaping government initiatives.
Experience with Q Research Software.
Strong proficiency in the Microsoft suite (PowerPoint, Excel, Word).

What we need from you:

Please click on the link and attach your resume (max 5 pages) and cover letter (max 2 pages). In your cover letter please share your motivation for applying for this position and your relevant skills.

Please note candidates invited to interview will be required to complete additional assessments.

A talent pool may be created from this recruitment process to fill future ongoing and temporary opportunities.

Salary Grade 11/12, with the base salary for this role starting at $149,739 base plus superannuation

Highly analytical and detail-oriented, with a proven ability to manage complex scope and competing priorities.

Proven competencies in: financial analysis, problem solving, attention to detail, adaptability, and excellent communication.

This is your chance to play a pivotal role in a major business transformation from the ground up. If you're ready to bring your energy, ideas, and initiative into a team that values Ownership, Teamwork, and Agility — we’d love to hear from you. Apply today and help shape the future of energy in Australia.

This is an outstanding opportunity to join a market leading and highly profitable Australian business which delivers immense value to its blue-chip client base domestically and globally. The organisation is highly driven to achieve ambitious outcomes, and needs aligned and high calibre professionals to join them.

Reporting to the Commercial Finance Director, this newly created role exists to partner closely with the Executive and business leaders across the company. The successful candidate will be able to support the development of commercial strategy, providing advice on pricing and highlighting financial and other performance metrics. You will support heads of departments and develop/monitor key performance indicators to improve business performance.

Specific accountabilities include:

Commercial Review and Development:

Ensure standard framework for pricing and pitch support.
Provide client profitability assessment and ongoing analysis.
Undertake division profitability assessment and ongoing analysis.
Conduct business case and ad hoc analysis.
Support client BD and RFP output.
Build commercial acumen at a business level.

Financial Planning and Analysis:

Lead all budgeting and forecasting activity for the Group.
Provide executive performance reporting to key stakeholders.
Oversee all group reporting requirements.
Undertake multi-year planning and analysis.
Prepare monthly board papers.
Manage and develop PowerBI dashboards.

The complexity, diversity and scale of the business will suit a driven and highly analytical, forward-looking finance leader with strong commercial acumen.  You will have gained strong business partnering and analytical experience in a professional or commercial B2B services environment. A team player with exceptional interpersonal skills, you are seeking a PE backed corporate culture which offers genuine scope for career challenges and growth. 

For a strictly confidential discussion, please contact Ian Hackett on 0408 312 216.  Alternatively, submit your application (Word version) via the Apply button.

Work options: Hybrid



About Us

We’re known for being the country’s most popular rewards program, helping over 10 million Australians get more of what they value beyond the checkout. And through our extended network of partnerships with other nationally recognised brands, we make every day simpler, easier and more rewarding for our members. Plus, we’re also the winner of Canstar Blue's 2023, 2024 and 2025 Most Satisfied Customer award for Rewards Programs 🥇🏆 

But, we're more than the card you carry around in your wallet or the app on your phone! Our diverse team is located all over Australia, and our incredible office is located right in the heart of Melbourne CBD. Staying true to our flexible working policy and people-centric culture, we ensure all our team members are set up to work remotely across Australia. We are outcomes-focused, and everything we do is aligned with our core principles - Own, Act and Inspire. 

Your Team

We've already built a pretty amazing team, but we're not looking for more of the same. Our day-to-day is made stronger when we surround ourselves with people who are different, like you.

Your Role

There is no typical working week at Flybuys. But here are some of the things you will be working on, day-to-day;

Develop and maintain the strategy for each Flybuys program partner aligned to the Flybuys corporate plan and partner commercial objectives.
Define strategic priorities, trade-offs and sequencing across short, medium and long term horizons.
Refresh partner strategies based on performance outcomes, market dynamics and partner priorities
Lead the Partner pillar of the Flybuys corporate plan, ensuring coherence across partners and consistency with enterprise priorities.
Develop the communications material using strong data support to frame the narrative to influence partner outcomes.
Identify cross‑partner opportunities that scale coalition value (e.g. shared mechanics, platforms, or propositions).
Surface strategic risks and dependencies relating to partners to ELT.
Define and evolve commercial constructs (e.g. funding models, incentives, qualification mechanics) in partnership with Finance and Partner Success.
Define partner‑level success measures aligned to strategy (member engagement, qualification efficiency, redemption behaviour, partner ROI).
Partner with Data & Insights to interpret performance, diagnose issues, and refine strategic direction.
Lead strategic discussions with partners in a collaborative, future‑focused manner.
Influence internal teams to align behind partner strategies and agreed priorities.
Prepare high‑impact materials for ELT, CEO, and Board discussions relating to partner strategy.
Bring forward new strategic ideas, propositions, or business models that unlock incremental value.

Your Experience

Tertiary Qualifications – ideally Business, Commerce, Law or equivalent business experience (MBA advantageous, but not essential).
3+ years’ experience at a top tier management consulting firm.
1-2+ years’ experience in external relationship management, with a track record of being able to influence outcomes.
A proven track record in leading strategic engagements, projects and managing and influencing multi-disciplinary project teams.
The ability to synthesize complex results and scenarios and communicate in a simple, but effective manner.
Demonstrated people management and leadership experience.
Budget management, with understanding of P&L and business reporting.
Experience working with Partners to co-design business solutions to drive customer engagement
Strong experience managing suppliers at all levels.
Experience of insight tools and services, using data to drive business outcomes.
A strategic thinking mindset, highly analytical in identifying problems to solve / opportunities to grow
·A natural passion for working as a team, but also the ability to work autonomously and own outcomes
